original: Eight-year-old girl finds pre-Viking-era sword in Vidöstern Lake, Sweden; internet proclaims her Queen of Sweden
Korean pop singerPsyreleases single "Gangnam Style," it becomes a worldwide hit, topping the charts in over 36 countries
"Stranger Things," created by the Duffer Brothers and starringWinona Ryder, David Harbour,Finn Wolfhard,Natalia Dyer, andMillie Bobby Brown, debuts on Netflix
"Harry Potterand the Half-Blood Prince," the sixth film based on the books byJ. K. Rowling, is released worldwide
The Rosetta Stone is found in the Egyptian village of Rosetta by French Captain Pierre-François Bouchard duringNapoleon's Egyptian Campaign
Mariner 4 sends back the first pictures of Mars
First duck-billed platypus publicly exhibited in the US at Bronx Zoological Park in New York City

American athleteJim Thorpeplaces in the top 4 in all 10 events, achieving an Olympic record of 8,413 points to win the Decathlon gold medal at the Stockholm Olympics; his medal is stripped in 1913 (played pro baseball) and reinstated in 1982
Adam Savage, American film special effects designer and television personality (MythBusters0, born in New York City
Wiley Postbegins the first solo flight around the world, completing the journey in 7 days and 19 hours
Sandhi Ortiz-DelValle is the first woman to officiate a men's pro basketball (USBL) game between New Haven Skyhawks and Philadelphia Spirit
A Russian Soyuz rocket with an international team launches for a mission to the International Space Station
Soyuz 19and Apollo 18 are launched; will rendezvous 2 days later
Columbia flies to Kennedy Space Center via Dyess AFB, Texas
Camillo Golgidiscovers the Golgi Apparatus (a delicate network inside cells essential for the transmission and reception of information between cells)
Howard Floreyand Norman Heatley present freeze-dried mold cultures of penicillin
FIFA World Cup Final, Luzhniki Stadium, Moscow, Russia: Former captainDidier Deschampsbecomes only the third man to win the event as a player and coach as France beats Croatia, 4-2
Filipino boxing legendManny Pacquiaostops WBA welterweight champion Lucas Matthysse in the seventh round in Kuala Lumpur for his first knockout in 9 years and 60th career victory
Tampa Bay catcher Travis d'Arnaud becomes the first player in MLB history to hit three home runs while catching and batting leadoff in the Rays' 5-4 win over the NY Yankees
California AngelNolan Ryan's pitches his second no-hitter, defeating the Detroit Tigers 6-0
Johnny Benchhits his 314th HR as a catcher which breaksYogi Berra's record
NY Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthties MLB record of 138 career home runs (held by Roger Connor since 1895) in 7-2 win over St. Louis Browns at Sportsman's Park, St. Louis
Krystyna Chojnowska-Liskiewicz, Polish engineer and sailor, first woman to sail single-handed around the world in 1978, born in Warsaw, Poland (d. 2021)
Enriqueta Basilio, Mexican track and field athlete, and first woman to light the Olympic Cauldron (Mexico City, 1968), born in Mexicali, Baja California (d. 2019)
World's first off-shore wind farm begins generating electricity off the coast of Vindeby, Denmark
Bandai volcano in Japan erupts for the first time in 1,000 years
Clara Adams is the first woman to complete a commercial round-the-world flight from New York, lasting 16 days and 19 hours
Chubby Checkerreleases his version of "The Twist" in the US (date approximate)
Clement Clarke Moore, US professor and author ('Twas the Night Before Xmas), born in New York City (d. 1863)
Maggie L. Walker, African-American teacher and businesswoman (1st female Bank President), born in Richmond, Virginia (d. 1934)
Aritana Yawalapiti, Brazilian indigenous cacique (leader) of Yawalapiti people, who sought to preserve the Amazon, born in Brazil (d. 2020)

Henry Johnson, American soldier of 369th regiment (aka Harlem Hell Fighters), 1st US WWI soldier to receive the Croix de guerre after fighting a German raid in hand-to-hand combat to rescue a fellow soldier (posthumous Medal of Honour, 2015), born in Tallahassee, Florida, (d. 1929)

World record rugby attendance of 108,874 sees New Zealand beat Australia 39-35 in a Tri Nations match at Stadium Australia, Sydney; five tries each with Andrew Mehrtens' goal-kicking (6) being the difference
Leon M. Lederman, American experimental physicist (Nobel Prize in Physics, 1988 - for research on quarks and leptons) and author (The God Particle), born in New York City (d. 2018)[1]
Carl Woese, American microbiologist (defining the Archaea) and academic, born in Syracuse, New York (d. 2012)
According to legend, English saint Swithun is reburied inside Winchester Cathedral against his wishes, and a terrible storm proceeds to rain for 40 days and 40 nights
Alexei Chirikov sights land in Southeast Alaska. He sends men ashore in a longboat, making them the first Europeans to visit Alaska
World's first steamboat the Pyroscaphe, built by Claude-François-Dorothée, marquis de Jouffroy d'Abbans, makes its first voyage on the river Saône in France

Physicist Donald Kerst is the first person to accelerate electrons using electromagnetic induction, reaching energies of 2.3 MeV, when his betatron device for particle acceleration becomes operational in Urbana, Illinois
Spain accepts equal rights for men and women

Scientists uncover possible reason for SS Waratah's disappearance 100 years later; The SS Waratah was a luxury steamer which did not reach its destination in 1909, and no trace of the wreckage was ever found
A 7.9 magnitude earthquake registers 160 km west of Invercargill, New Zealand, creating a small tsunami

Battle of Grunwald (First Battle of Tannenburg, Battle of Žalgiris), one of Medieval Europe's largest battles during the Polish-Lithuanian Teutonic War. Polish King Władysław Jagiełło and Lithuanian Grand Duke Vytautas defeat Teutonic Ulrich von Jungingen.
John Wesley Work III, African-American composer, musicologist and scholar of African-American folklore and music, born in Tullahoma, Tennessee (d. 1967)
Akhtar Hameed Khan, Pakistani pioneer of Microcredit in developing countries, born in Agra, British India (d. 1999)
Trevor Horn, British rock musician and record producer (Buggles - "Video Killed The Radio Star"; Yes - Drama; Frankie Goes To Hollywood), born in Durham, England
SingerEd Sheeranwelcomes surprise guestEminemfor two duets during his concert at Ford Field in Detroit, Michigan
Rijksmuseum opens Rembrandt Hall in Amsterdam
Diligenti quintuplets, born in Buenos Aires, Argentina
Portuguese explorer Pedro Cabral arrives back in Lisbon from his voyage to India, having lost six ships, during which he accidently discovered Brazil
Zebulon Pike begins his journey to explore the American Southwest
Napoleonsurrenders to Captain Frederick Maitland of HMS Bellerophon at Rochefort after his earlier defeat at the Battle of Waterloo
46 inches of rain (beginning 7/14) falls in Baguio, Philippines
First transatlantic helicopter flight begins
110°F (43°C) recorded at Balcony Falls, Virginia (state record)
Commercial air travel between US and USSR begins
Heavy monsoon flooding in India's northeastern state of Assam affects more than 2 million people and kills more than 50
Leonard Bernsteinpremieres his "Chichester Psalms" for chorus and orchestra at the Philharmonic Hall, New York City
"Honky Chateau" becomesElton John's first No. 1 album in the US, includes hit "Rocket Man"
Frances Xavier Cabrini [Mother Cabrini], Italian-American saint (1st American saint, founded Missionary Sisters of the Sacred Heart), born in Sant’Angelo Lodigiano, Lombardy (d. 1917)
